english: interrupting and postponing it to a more convenient time.

resp. sku-skál (sku skal) 1. portion, share; bgo-skál (bgo skal) allotted portion; zas-skál (zas skal) portion of food, ration; raṅ-skál (rang skal) personal share; nor-skál (nor skal) or syal-nór (syal nor) Glr. hereditary portion, inheritance; skál-ba ma čád-par (skal ba ma chad par) without being shortened of his portion Mil.; ma mṭóṅ-ba skál-ba ma mčis-pa _dra (ma mthong ba skal ba ma mchis pa 'dra) it does not seem to fall to my lot to see my mother. — skal-čád (skal chad) dried up, withered Sch. (?) — 2. in a special sense: the portion of good or bad fortune that falls to a man's lot, as a consequence of his former actions, lot, fate, destiny, a. relatively: skál-ba bzáṅ-po (skal ba bzang po), ṅán-pa (ngan pa) good, bad fortune; tse _dii grogs-s'ál (tshe 'di'i grogs s'al) the matrimonial share of the present life, the connubial fate for which a person is predestinated Glr. (The Buddhist priests pretend to be able to calculate the skál-ba (skal ba) of any one after his death) b. in a positive and good sense, denoting either prosperity and blessing as a consequence of good actions, or those actions themselves as being pious and meritorious, so that skal-ldán (skal ldan) means happy, blessed as well as pious, devout, and skal-méd (skal med) unhappy, irreligious, impious. skal-ldán (skal ldan) are all those who have devoted themselves to virtue and treasured up more or less good works, and who may expect to be promoted in proportion. The term worthy, therefore, though not quite correct as to the word itself, is still very appropriate as it regards the subject; even venerable, holy may be applied occasionally, cf. भगवत् and भगवान्. Also some single blessing or spiritual gift may be meant by skál-ba (skal ba) and so the Ommanipadmehūm is called the čos-skál (chos skal), 'the religious treasure', of Tibet Glr.

B., C., skás-Ka (skas kha), skás-pa (skas pa) C., skrás-ka (skras ka) (pronounced *tẹ́-ka (te ka) C., śrás-ka (shras ka), śré-ka (shre ka) W.), even skas (skas), skad (skad) ladder, generally consisting of the notched trunk of a tree; rkyáṅ-skad (rkyang skad) C. 'single ladder', the same, compared with two or three of them joined together, to make a sort of staircase with broader steps; *do-tá (do ta) C., do-śrás (do shras), do-śrẹ́* (do shre) W. a flight of stone-steps; *gya-śrás (gya shras), gya-śré (gya shre) W., gya-kẹ́* (gya ke) C. (Schr.) a regular staircase as in European houses; gru-skás (gru skas) Glr. prob.: flight of steps at the corner of a building; gro-skad (gro skad) Glr. fol. 7 appeared to be unknown to those that were consulted; skas-kyi rim-pa (skas kyi rim pa) Cs. steps; *śra-ldaṅ (shra ldang), śral-dáṅ* (shral dang) W. spokes of a ladder; skas _gram (skas 'gram) Cs. the two side-pieces of a staircase or ladder; skas _dzug-pa (skas 'dzug pa) to apply a ladder Schr., Cs.

1. also sku-lus (sku lus), sku-yzugs (sku gzugs), resp. for lus (lus): body; by being prefixed to the names of parts of the body and even of everything that has reference to the bodily existence of a person, it imparts to them the character of respectful terms: sku-stod (sku stod), -smad (-smad) the upper, lower, part of the body; sku-śa (sku sha) flesh; sku-mṭsál (sku mtshal) (for sku-Krag (sku khrag)) blood Cs.; sku-mdóg (sku mdog) colour of the skin, sku-ná (sku na) age; sku-tse (sku tshe) lifetime, life; sku-Káms (sku khams) state of health; sku-skál (sku skal) portion, share, sku-čás (sku chas) goods, stores Mil., sku-bsód (sku bsod) virtue, happiness Tar.; sku-skyés (sku skyes) a present (given to or received from a respected personage); sku- _bág (sku 'bag) image, statue Glr.; sku-mdun-pa (sku mdun pa) (C.: *kun-dym-pa* (kun dym pa) or -druṅ-pa (-drung pa)) attendant of a man of rank; *Ku-jar-wa* (khu jar wa) ('adherent', v. _byar-ba ('byar ba)) id. C.; sku-nye (sku nye) Sch. relation, kinsman; sku-yśegs-pa (sku gshegs pa) dying, death (of a king etc.) Glr.; sku-bstod (sku bstod) praise Sch.; sku-śogs (sku shogs) (acc. to Cs. instead of sku yśogs (sku gshogs) 'the side' = your presence) a title of honour, when we should say: your or his honour, your or his worship, in W. only for clerical dignitaries, in C. also for other persons of rank. Even buildings (monasteries etc.) are honoured by these respectful expressions: sku-dkar ysol-ba (sku dkar gsol ba) to 'administer' whitewash — 2. in a special sense: the person of Buddha, whom philosophers represent in three forms of existence called sku-ysum (sku gsum) त्रिकाय, viz.: čós-kyi sku (chos kyi sku), धर्मकाय, loṅs-spyód-kyi sku (longs spyod kyi sku) संभोगकाय and sprúl-pai sku (sprul pa'i sku) निर्माणकाय. These three 'persons', however, have as little as dkon-mčog-ysum (dkon mchog gsum)
